subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV, World Bank, EBRD, V-Dem, RSF, TI, Freedom House) + Armenian independent press (CivilNet, EVN Report, Hetq) + regional (Eurasianet, OC Media).
A reforming post-2018 democracy under acute geopolitical threat after the 2023 loss of Nagorno-Karabakh, pivoting from Russia toward the West while still Russia-energy-dependent.

Standout drags: an unresolved/coerced peace and blockade, social-cohesion trauma, and a risky Russia-to-West realignment; a free-ish press, prudent macro, and absorbed refugees/Russians offset. Source-lean: MODERATE Anglophone.

economic.notes: Prudent macro and a credible central bank, with the Russian-inflow boom normalizing into structural blockade exposure.

fiscal_state.notes: Moderate debt (~47% GDP) and a 2025 deficit below budget, with a new IMF arrangement. (IMF 2025 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: A credible inflation-targeting central bank, managed and with no peg. (CBA; IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The dram is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: ~3-4% and near the target band. (CBA; World Bank)

banking_stability.notes: Sound and well-capitalised, boosted by 2022-23 Russian inflows. (IMF; EBRD)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: IT/tech and copper, but landlocked and blockaded by two neighbours and Russia-trade-dependent, with the re-export boom now declining. (IMF; Eurasianet)

institutional.notes: A reforming but weak rule of law, with a free-ish but eroding press.

rule_of_law.notes: A post-2018 democracy with an anti-corruption drive, but weak institutions and politicised 2025-26 prosecutions. (V-Dem; Freedom House 2026; CivilNet)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Reforming and mixed, with brain-drain risk. (World Bank; EBRD)

press_freedom.notes: A regional bright spot (RSF ~50th) but deteriorating amid polarisation. (RSF 2026; Hetq)

political_social.notes: A fragile reforming democracy carrying the Karabakh-loss trauma and refugee strain.

political_stability.notes: A competitive 2026 election with an unpopular incumbent, 2025 coup-plot arrests, polarised and fragile - net mild-positive on democratic continuity. (Eurasianet; OBC Transeuropa)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Absorbed ~100k Karabakh Armenians and 2022 Russians; diaspora-friendly, lawful routes. (Eurasianet; UNHCR)

social_cohesion.notes: Karabakh-loss trauma, polarisation, and refugee-integration strain. (OC Media; EVN Report)

demographics.notes: Small (~3M), ageing, with sustained emigration. (World Bank; UNFPA)

geopolitical.notes: A risky Russia-to-West realignment under acute, unresolved conflict exposure.

alliance_reliability.notes: Froze CSTO membership and pursues EU accession and US ties - a risky realignment, still Russia-energy/trade-dependent. (Bloomberg; Carnegie)

conflict_involvement.notes: Defeated in 2023 with a coerced/unresolved peace process, blockaded, and a residual war risk. (European Parliament; ICG)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Open and not sanctioned, but with re-export/circumvention scrutiny. (US State; IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Major seismic risk and blockaded, energy-dependent geography, with low crime a strength.

climate.notes: Mid-low vulnerability, moderate readiness - warming, water stress, agricultural exposure in the landlocked South Caucasus. (ND-GAIN; IPCC AR6)

healthcare.notes: Mixed and improving but uneven, with seismic-vulnerable facilities. (WHO; World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: Improving but landlocked/blockaded and energy-dependent on Russia and Iran. (IEA; IWPR)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low penetration against high seismic exposure. (GFDRR)

summary: A reforming post-2018 democracy with prudent macro management, a credible central bank, low inflation, a free-ish press, and an openness that absorbed both Karabakh refugees and Russians fleeing in 2022. But it is under acute geopolitical strain: defeated by Azerbaijan in 2023 with the loss of Nagorno-Karabakh, blockaded by two neighbours, energy-dependent on Russia and Iran, and now attempting a risky pivot from Russia toward the EU and the West. Social-cohesion trauma, an unresolved/coerced peace with residual war risk, major seismic exposure, and emigration leave the outcome distribution negative.
